command: $doms=(Get-DnsClientCache).Name | Where-Object { ($_ -split '\.')[0].Length -gt 12 } | Select-Object -Unique; $stats=@(); foreach($d in $doms){ $fails=0; 1..3 | ForEach-Object { try{ $r=Invoke-WebRequest -UseBasicParsing -Headers @{accept='application/dns-json'} "https://dns.google/resolve?name=$d&type=A"; if(((($r.Content|ConvertFrom-Json).Status) -ne 0)){ $fails++ } } catch { $fails++ } }; $stats+=[pscustomobject]@{Domain=$d; NX=$fails} }; $flag=$stats | Where-Object { $_.NX -ge 2 } | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Domain; if($flag){ Invoke-WebRequest -UseBasicParsing -Method POST -ContentType 'application/json' -Body (@{ts=(Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString('o'); suspected=$flag}|ConvertTo-Json) 'https://siem.example.local/api/ingest/nxdomain-suspects' }
explanation: Defensive NXDOMAIN scoring for T1568: evaluates repetitive NXDOMAIN responses for suspicious domains, which is common in DGA/dynamic resolution probing.
rationale_steps: Select high-suspicion domains from cache by length.
Resolve each domain multiple times via DoH.
Report domains with high NXDOMAIN ratios to SIEM.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed PowerShell with valid cmdlets/parameters and balanced quoting; the over-parenthesized if condition is slightly awkward but likely still parseable.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It collects candidate domains from the DNS client cache, probes them via DNS-over-HTTPS, scores repeated failures, and reports suspects to a SIEM—consistent with defensive detection of dynamic resolution/DGA behavior, though it flags any non-zero DNS status (not strictly NXDOMAIN) and uses a placeholder SIEM endpoint.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is largely well-formed PowerShell with balanced braces, proper pipeline usage, and valid cmdlet syntax, though the nested string interpolation in the Invoke-WebRequest URI (unquoted URL at the end) and complex quoting within the foreach loop could cause edge-case parsing issues in some environments.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command correctly implements a defensive T1568 detection workflow by extracting long-label domains from the DNS client cache (a valid DGA heuristic), querying Google's DoH API to score NXDOMAIN failures, and reporting suspects to a SIEM endpoint, which is operationally plausible and well-aligned with detecting DGA/dynamic resolution activity, though querying the local DNS cache rather than live traffic limits detection coverage.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is a perfectly well-formed PowerShell script with correct variable scoping, balanced delimiters, and valid use of cmdlets and pipelines.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command effectively implements a defensive hunting logic for DGA activity by filtering the DNS cache for high-entropy strings, verifying their resolution status via an external API, and reporting suspected NXDOMAIN churn to a SIEM.
